graphite-ink.$description: Graphite Ink — Primary text, headings, nav links, list markers — near-black with a faint cool cast gives ink-like density without the harshness of pure #000

hairline-slate.$description: Hairline Slate — Borders, dividers, card outlines, image frames — the workhorse neutral that defines structural edges across every surface

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Page canvas, card surfaces, text on dark fills — the base layer everything sits on

carbon.$description: Carbon — Secondary text, monospace body fills, subtle iconography — slightly warmer than graphite for code and terminal contexts

mist.$description: Mist — Subtle surface lift, ghost button borders, soft section backgrounds — the second tier above pure white

obsidian.$description: Obsidian — High-contrast neutral action fill for primary buttons on light surfaces.

iris-glow.$description: Iris Glow — Supporting palette color for small decorative accents when the core palette needs contrast. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

magenta-pulse.$description: Magenta Pulse — Supporting palette color for small decorative accents when the core palette needs contrast. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

ember-orange.$description: Ember Orange — Supporting palette color for small decorative accents when the core palette needs contrast.

rose-hot.$description: Rose Hot — Red text accent for links, tags, and emphasized short phrases.

geistsans.$description: Primary UI and display typeface. The 76px display size with -0.046em tracking creates a compressed, architectural headline that spans three lines without overwhelming the viewport. Geist's geometric construction gives a monospace-adjacent precision that fits the developer-tools context without committing to mono. 400 is the body workhorse, 500 carries nav and button labels, 600 is reserved for emphasis within dense lists.

geistmono.$description: Code and terminal mockup typeface — used inside the hero terminal window and any inline code blocks. Single weight 500 keeps the monospace voice present but quiet, never competing with the display sans.

paper-white.$description: Surface level 0: Page canvas — the flat base everything sits on

mist.$description: Surface level 1: Subtle lift for alt sections, ghost button surfaces, code block backgrounds

obsidian.$description: Surface level 2: Inverted dark surface for terminal windows and solid dark action buttons
